brief_justification: Agent concludes radioactive isotopes unambiguously ARE elements (matching correct answer YES) by rejecting the false criterion; goes three levels deep: IUPAC definition → equivocation diagnosis → type/token distinction and reductio ad absurdum; explicitly announces 'let me anchor key terms before reasoning' and tracks drift step by step; applies boundary-condition verification (permanence criterion makes ALL elements fail, vacuous category); clearly separates the question's proposed criterion from the actual operational definition; evaluates the permanence framing seriously, then rejects it with three distinct arguments; every conclusion traces to an explicit derivation — proton count → instantaneous identity → type vs. token; avoids the negative gate pattern of assumed definition stability — instead actively detects and names the substitution; zero evidence of assumed_definition_stability or implicit_meaning_inheritance, explicitly addresses both suppressions; follows topology almost exactly: freeze definitions → anchor → monitor drift → classify as silent equivocation → force re-anchor → verify no valid conclusion rests on the slippage
